symptoms.symptom_name: Persistent bleeding from a forehead lesion after minor trauma, failing prolonged direct pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Basal cell carcinoma with acute haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A friable, ulcerated tumour bed bleeds readily and resists normal primary haemostasis.
A simple wound in a person not on anticoagulants would usually arrest with prolonged pressure; failure points to an abnormal lesional bed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Six-month slowly enlarging, painless lesion on chronically sun-exposed skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Indolent, painless growth over months on sun-exposed skin is the classic nodular-BCC natural history.
Also arises on sun-damaged skin, though usually faster-growing and hyperkeratotic.
Can present as a slowly growing non-pigmented nodule; kept in play as an important-not-to-miss mimic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Shiny/pale nodule with a small central ulcer that bleeds easily (lay-observable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Shiny nodule with central ulceration that bleeds is the patient-visible face of a rodent ulcer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pearly dome-shaped papule with raised rolled borders and arborising telangiectasia; 1.5 cm; central rodent ulceration oozing venous blood

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pearly rolled border plus arborising telangiectasia on dermoscopy is highly characteristic of nodular BCC — but this is a clinician/dermoscopic finding the AI cannot obtain.
SCC is typically hyperkeratotic/scaly rather than pearly and telangiectatic.
Absent nodes is consistent with BCC (which rarely metastasises); palpation requires an in-person examiner.

symptoms.symptom_name: No anticoagulant or antiplatelet use (aspirin/clopidogrel/warfarin/DOAC negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Basal cell carcinoma with acute haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: With no systemic anticoagulation, failure of pressure to arrest bleeding is attributable to the local tumour bed rather than a coagulopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of dizziness, faintness or lightheadedness (no haemodynamic compromise)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Basal cell carcinoma with acute haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of orthostatic/hypovolaemic symptoms keeps the encounter at urgent same-day rather than emergency acuity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs stable (BP 135/85, HR 95, SpO2 98%, afebrile 36.7)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Basal cell carcinoma with acute haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured stability confirms modest blood loss; the AI cannot obtain these values without a home device and infers stability from the absence of reported symptoms.

investigation_details.expected_result: Nests of basaloid cells with peripheral palisading and retraction artefact — confirms basal cell carcinoma and subtype
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Malignancy remains unconfirmed and the definitive excision plan cannot be finalised.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Histopathology is the only definitive means of confirming malignancy and distinguishing BCC from SCC or amelanotic melanoma — never available over telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Nodular BCC morphology on sun-exposed skin
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A slow-growing painless shiny ulcerating nodule on chronically sun-exposed skin is the classic clinical picture — strongly suspicious but still requiring histological confirmation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person wound assessment and definitive haemostasis (chemical/physical cautery + dressing)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Active bleeding failing home pressure needs a clinician to secure haemostasis today; the AI cannot perform this.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Dermoscopy of the lesion by a clinician/dermatologist
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterises the lesion and guides biopsy/excision; not obtainable over chat.
Definitive confirmation of malignancy and subtype ahead of complete surgical excision.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine full blood count / coagulation screen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No anticoagulant use and no systemic bleeding features — routine bloods are not indicated for a local tumour-bed ooze and should not be over-ordered.
